name: San Cibrao - return via Xunco river and Old Sargadelos porcelain factory
name_original: San Cibrao – vuelta por río Xunco y Antiga fábrica de louza de Sargadelos
region_intro: Touring cycling around Costa features a diverse landscape characterized by its Atlantic coastline, estuaries (rías), and a green, hilly interior. The region offers routes that traverse coastal paths, follow river valleys, and wind through rural areas. Cyclists can expect a mix of flat sections along the coast and more challenging climbs inland. This area provides varied terrain for different cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Costa The most popular touring cycling route is Ribadeo – tour around Ría de Foz – Special Conservation Area Ría de Foz-Masma, a 29.2 miles (46.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours to complete. This route explores the scenic Ría de Foz and its conservation area. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Viveiro – tour around Paseo Marítimo and Ría de Viveiro, a moderate 8.6 miles (13.8 km) path. This route offers views of the Viveiro estuary and its coastal promenade. Local touring cyclists also love the Foz – return via Ponte Vella of Rio Ouro, a 9.1 miles (14.7 km) trail leading through river scenery and coastal areas, often completed in about 1 hour 2 minutes. Touring cycling around Costa is defined by coastal paths, river valleys, and estuaries, offering a mix of flat and hilly terrain. The network provides options for different ability levels, from moderate coastal rides to more difficult inland routes. The routes in Costa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Costa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Costa?
A: There are over 170 touring cycling routes available in Costa, offering a diverse range of experiences from coastal paths to hilly inland trails. The komoot community has explored more than 2000 of these routes.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Costa?
A: Yes, Costa offers 22 easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners. These routes typically feature flatter sections along the coast or gentle river valleys, providing a relaxed introduction to the region's scenery.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while touring cycling in Costa?
A: Touring cycling in Costa offers a diverse landscape characterized by its Atlantic coastline, picturesque estuaries (rías), and a green, hilly interior. You can expect routes that traverse coastal paths, follow river valleys, and wind through rural areas, providing a mix of flat sections and more challenging climbs.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Costa?
A: Yes, many routes in Costa are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017423035/burela-loop-via-covo-river-and-marosa-beach" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burela - loop via Covo river and Marosa Beach&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging 30.8 km ride, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31917717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach of the Cathedrals – Ribadeo loop from Barreiros&lt;/a&gt; provides a moderate coastal experience.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in Costa?
A: For those seeking a challenge, Costa has 107 difficult touring cycling routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017428671/mondonedo-tour-around-the-monastery-of-san-salvador-and-trabada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondoñedo – tour around the Monastery of San Salvador and Trabada&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 48 km with significant elevation gain, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017363004/mondonedo-return-via-tronceda-river-waterfalls" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondoñedo – return via Tronceda river Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes that feature waterfalls in Costa?
A: Yes, Costa features several beautiful waterfalls. You can explore routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017363004/mondonedo-return-via-tronceda-river-waterfalls" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondoñedo – return via Tronceda river Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tronceda River Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable waterfalls in the region include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291168" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto do Coro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4632183" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de Lexoso&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore along the touring cycling routes in Costa?
A: Beyond the scenic rías and river valleys, you can discover impressive natural monuments. The iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/526099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach of the Cathedrals&lt;/a&gt; is a must-see, known for its stunning rock formations. Other highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3163847" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de los Castros Natural Rock Arch&lt;/a&gt; and the panoramic views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/284283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuciño do Porco Point&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the average duration for touring cycling routes in Costa?
A: The duration of touring cycling routes in Costa varies widely depending on the distance and difficulty. Moderate routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017392599/viveiro-tour-around-paseo-maritimo-and-ria-de-viveiro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viveiro – tour around Paseo Marítimo and Ría de Viveiro&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about an hour, while longer, more challenging routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017400036/ribadeo-tour-around-ria-de-foz-special-conservation-area-ria-de-foz-masma" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribadeo – tour around Ría de Foz – Special Conservation Area Ría de Foz-Masma&lt;/a&gt; may take around 3 hours or more.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on touring cycling routes in Costa?
A: While specific wildlife encounters depend on the route and season, the green, natural landscapes of Costa, particularly around estuaries and river valleys, are home to diverse birdlife and small mammals. Keep an eye out for various waterfowl along the rías and local fauna in the forested inland areas.

Q: How do other cyclists rate the touring cycling routes in Costa?
A: The touring cycling routes in Costa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an impressive average score of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, scenic coastal views, and the well-maintained paths that make for an enjoyable experience.

Q: Are there any routes that offer a mix of river and coastal scenery?
A: Absolutely! Many routes in Costa combine both river and coastal elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017395755/foz-return-via-ponte-vella-of-rio-ouro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz – return via Ponte Vella of Rio Ouro&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, leading you through river scenery before reaching coastal areas. Similarly, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017417411/san-cibrao-return-via-xunco-river-and-old-sargadelos-porcelain-factory" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Cibrao - return via Xunco river and Old Sargadelos porcelain factory&lt;/a&gt; offers a blend of river and historical coastal sights.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Costa?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Costa generally aligns with the drier and milder months. While specific regional weather can vary, spring and early autumn often offer pleasant temperatures and less rainfall, making for ideal cycling conditions along both the coast and inland.
tips: From the Covo river bridge you can see the San Cibrao view with its row of buildings on the riverbank and the boats along the boardwalk.
Neat white-washed church serving the little community on the west bank of the Covo river.
Recreational path along the Cervo river. There are ruins of the buildings of an old porcelain factory and a museum dedicated to the Sargadelos factory.
Sandy cove on the east side of the narrow peninsula

waypoints.name: Ruins of the Sargadelos Porcelain Factory
